pois.name: Brandenburger Tor
pois.description: The only remaining city gate of Berlin and a national symbol of German unity and peace. Built in the late 18th century, it stands at the end of Unter den Linden.

pois.name: Reichstagsgebäude
pois.description: The historic seat of the German Parliament featuring a modern glass dome by Norman Foster. It has been a central witness to German history since its completion in 1894.

pois.name: Museumsinsel
pois.description: A UNESCO World Heritage site comprising five world-renowned museums on a small island in the Spree river. It houses massive archaeological collections including the Pergamon Altar.

pois.name: Berliner Dom
pois.description: The largest Protestant church in Germany, known for its massive dome and the Hohenzollern Crypt. The current building was completed in 1905 in Neo-Renaissance style.

pois.name: Gendarmenmarkt
pois.description: Often described as the most beautiful square in Berlin, featuring the Concert House and the twin French and German Cathedrals. It dates back to the late 17th century.

pois.name: Nikolaiviertel
pois.description: The oldest residential area in Berlin, reconstructed around the medieval St. Nicholas' Church. It retains a unique historic atmosphere despite heavy wartime damage.

pois.name: Schloss Charlottenburg
pois.description: The largest palace in Berlin, originally built as a summer residence for Sophie Charlotte. It features extensive Baroque and Rococo state rooms and a vast garden.

pois.name: Kaiser-Wilhelm-Gedächtniskirche
pois.description: A ruined spire left as a memorial to peace and reconciliation following Allied bombing in WWII. A modern hexagonal bell tower and chapel stand adjacent to the ruins.

pois.name: Siegessäule
pois.description: The Victory Column was originally built to commemorate Prussian victories in the unification wars. Visitors can climb to the top for a panoramic view of the Tiergarten.

pois.name: Zitadelle Spandau
pois.description: One of the best-preserved Renaissance military structures in Europe. It features the 13th-century Julius Tower and houses various cultural museums.

pois.name: Schloss Köpenick
pois.description: A Baroque palace located on an island in the Dahme river, surrounded by a charming park. It houses the Kunstgewerbemuseum's collection of decorative arts.

pois.name: Pfaueninsel
pois.description: A romantic island on the Havel accessible only by ferry, famous for its free-roaming peacocks and a whimsical white castle. It is a UNESCO World Heritage site.

pois.name: Schloss Glienicke
pois.description: A Mediterranean-style villa designed by Karl Friedrich Schinkel for Prince Carl of Prussia. It offers views of the Glienicke Bridge and the Havel landscape.

pois.name: Jagdschloss Grunewald
pois.description: The oldest surviving palace in Berlin, built in Renaissance style as a hunting lodge. It sits on the shore of Grunewaldsee and displays a fine Cranach collection.

pois.name: Oberbaumbrücke
pois.description: A double-deck bridge with brick Gothic turrets connecting Friedrichshain and Kreuzberg. It became a prominent symbol of Berlin's reunification after the Wall fell.

pois.name: East Side Gallery
pois.description: A 1.3-kilometer stretch of the Berlin Wall covered in murals by international artists. It serves as an open-air gallery and a memorial to freedom.

pois.name: Checkpoint Charlie
pois.description: The most famous border crossing between East and West Berlin during the Cold War. A replica of the original guard house stands at the site today.

pois.name: Denkmal für die ermordeten Juden Europas
pois.description: A massive field of 2,711 concrete slabs of varying heights, creating an uneasy atmosphere. It serves as the central Holocaust memorial in Germany.

pois.name: Sowjetisches Ehrenmal im Treptower Park
pois.description: A monumental military cemetery and memorial for the Soviet soldiers who fell during the Battle of Berlin. It features a colossal statue of a soldier with a child.

pois.name: Teufelsberg
pois.description: A man-made hill built from WWII rubble, formerly the site of a US listening station. It offers iconic views and is covered in extensive street art.

pois.name: Müggelturm
pois.description: An observation tower on the Müggelberge hills providing the best views over the lakes of southeastern Berlin. The current concrete structure dates to 1961.

pois.name: Großer Müggelsee
pois.description: The largest lake in Berlin, a popular destination for water sports and recreation in the Köpenick district. It is fed by the Spree river.

pois.name: Großer Wannsee
pois.description: A large bight of the Havel river famous for its beach lido and the historic villas lining its shores. It is a central part of Berlin's sailing culture.

pois.name: Tegeler See
pois.description: The second-largest lake in Berlin, located in the Reinickendorf district and known for its seven islands and the Greenwichpromenade.

pois.name: Viktoriapark Wasserfall
pois.description: A man-made 24-meter waterfall in Kreuzberg, designed to mimic the landscape of the Riesengebirge. It leads up to a monument designed by Schinkel.

pois.name: St. Marienkirche
pois.description: One of the oldest churches in Berlin, featuring a famous 15th-century fresco titled 'The Dance of Death'. It stands near the Alexanderplatz.

pois.name: St. Hedwigs-Kathedrale
pois.description: The seat of the Roman Catholic Archbishop of Berlin, notable for its large copper dome inspired by the Pantheon in Rome.

pois.name: Schloss Britz
pois.description: A former manor house in Neukölln, once the seat of the counts of Hertzberg. It now serves as a cultural venue with a well-preserved rose garden.

pois.name: Dorfkirche Dahlem
pois.description: A small, picturesque medieval stone church in the heart of the Dahlem village. It is one of the oldest sacred buildings in the city.

pois.name: Domäne Dahlem
pois.description: An open-air museum of agrarian history located on the site of a former knight's manor. It features historic farm buildings and traditional craft workshops.

pois.name: Kulturbrauerei
pois.description: A massive 19th-century industrial complex that was once the Schultheiss brewery. It now serves as a major cultural center with cinemas, clubs, and theaters.

pois.name: Prater Garten
pois.description: The oldest beer garden in Berlin, founded in 1837. It has survived the industrial revolution and multiple political regimes as a social hub in Prenzlauer Berg.

pois.name: Brauhaus Lemke am Schloss
pois.description: A traditional brewery and tavern located directly opposite Charlottenburg Palace, known for its local Berlin-style beers and rustic food.

pois.name: Jungfernbrücke
pois.description: The oldest bridge in Berlin, a cast-iron bascule bridge spanning the Spree Canal. It is a rare survivor of the city's early engineering works.

pois.name: Moltkebrücke
pois.description: A red sandstone bridge decorated with sculptures and reliefs, spanning the Spree near the main railway station. It was a site of fierce fighting in 1945.

pois.name: Glienicker Brücke
pois.description: Famous as the 'Bridge of Spies' for the exchange of prisoners between East and West during the Cold War. It connects Berlin with Potsdam.

pois.name: Berliner Fernsehturm
pois.description: The tallest structure in Germany, built by the GDR as a symbol of socialist power. The observation deck offers 360-degree views of the entire city.

pois.name: Flakturm Humboldthain
pois.description: The remains of a massive WWII anti-aircraft bunker and tower. Only partially demolished, it now provides a vantage point and a site for guided underworld tours.

pois.name: Anhalter Bahnhof Ruine
pois.description: The fragment of the portico of what was once Berlin's grandest railway station. It stands as a haunting reminder of the city's pre-war scale and wartime destruction.

pois.name: Französischer Dom
pois.description: Built by the Huguenot community, this church features a prominent domed tower added later. It houses the Huguenot Museum and a carillon.

pois.name: Deutscher Dom
pois.description: Located on Gendarmenmarkt opposite the French Cathedral, it now serves as a museum dedicated to the history of parliamentary democracy in Germany.

pois.name: St. Nikolai-Kirche Spandau
pois.description: A three-aisled hall church in Spandau's old town, notable for its Renaissance font and Baroque altar. It was a site of the Reformation's introduction in Brandenburg.

pois.name: Alt-Marzahn
pois.description: A preserved village green within the modern Marzahn district, featuring historic farmhouses and a peaceful rural atmosphere amidst high-rise apartments.

pois.name: Bockwindmühle Marzahn
pois.description: A functional traditional post mill in the village of Marzahn. It is a reconstruction that demonstrates historic milling techniques to visitors.

pois.name: Schloss Friedrichsfelde
pois.description: An early neoclassical palace located inside the Tierpark Berlin zoo. It was once a residence for Prussian nobility and now hosts concerts and exhibitions.

pois.name: Schloss Biesdorf
pois.description: A late-classical villa known for its distinctive octagonal tower and red terracotta facade. It serves as a municipal gallery for urban and regional art.

pois.name: Grunewaldturm
pois.description: A brick Gothic Revival tower built to honor Emperor Wilhelm I. It stands on the Karlsberg hill and provides views over the Havel river landscape.

pois.name: Gedenkstätte Berliner Mauer
pois.description: The central memorial site of German division, featuring a preserved section of the border fortifications and a documentation center.

pois.name: Neue Synagoge
pois.description: A major center of Jewish life in Berlin with an iconic gilded dome. Heavily damaged during the Kristallnacht and WWII, it has been partially restored.

pois.name: Topographie des Terrors
pois.description: An outdoor and indoor history museum on the site of the former SS and Gestapo headquarters. It documents the crimes of the Nazi regime.

pois.name: Bendlerblock
pois.description: A building complex that was the headquarters of the resistance group around Claus von Stauffenberg. It now houses the Memorial to the German Resistance.

pois.name: Franziskaner-Klosterkirche Ruin
pois.description: The ruins of a Franciscan monastery church built in the 13th century. It remains one of the most important Gothic brick buildings in the city.

pois.name: Sophienkirche
pois.description: A Baroque parish church in the Spandauer Vorstadt, notable for its ornate tower and for being the site of a famous sermon by Martin Luther King Jr.

pois.name: Zur letzten Instanz
pois.description: Berlin's oldest restaurant, established in 1621. Its wood-paneled interior and historic guest list, including Napoleon, make it a landmark of Berlin culinary history.

pois.name: Clärchens Ballhaus
pois.description: A legendary dance hall and restaurant that has survived two world wars. Its Mirror Hall is a rare example of pre-war Berlin ballroom opulence.

pois.name: Schloss Bellevue
pois.description: The official residence of the President of Germany since 1994. It was built in 1786 as a summer palace for Prince Augustus Ferdinand of Prussia.

pois.name: Böhmisches Dorf Rixdorf
pois.description: A village within the city founded by Bohemian refugees in the 18th century. It retains a cobblestone charm and hosts a famous traditional Christmas market.

pois.name: Jüdischer Friedhof Weißensee
pois.description: One of the largest and most beautiful Jewish cemeteries in Europe, featuring over 115,000 graves and impressive mausoleums in various architectural styles.

pois.name: Grunewaldsee
pois.description: A forest lake located in the Grunewald forest, popular for swimming and dog walking, adjacent to Berlin's oldest hunting lodge.

pois.name: Großer Stein
pois.description: A massive glacial erratic boulder located near the banks of the Müggelspree. It is an ice-age monument brought here from Scandinavia by glaciers.
